name: Veeva Vault REST API
what_it_does: Veeva Vault REST API for cloud-based content management platform for life sciences and regulated industries. Enables AI agents to manage clinical, regulatory, quality, and commercial content lifecycle automation, handle document version control and approval workflow management, access clinical trial data and eTMF (electronic Trial Master File) document management, retrieve regulatory submission document management and eCTD tracking, manage quality management and CAPA workflow automation, handle pharmacovigilance and safety document management, access medical affairs and content review workflows, retrieve audit trail and compliance reporting data, manage vault cross-link data object relationships, and integrate Veeva Vault with CTMS, LIMS, safety systems, and ERP platforms.
use_cases: Automating regulatory submission document workflows from regulatory agents
Managing clinical trial document management from eTMF agents
Handling quality management CAPA workflows from quality system agents
Integrating Vault data with CTMS from clinical operations agents
not_for: General document management without life sciences GxP compliance requirements
Consumer document storage without enterprise regulatory validation requirements
Simple CMS without life sciences content lifecycle management
best_when: A life sciences company using Veeva Vault wants AI agents to automate document workflows, regulatory submission tracking, clinical content management, CAPA workflows, and system integration.
avoid_when: CRITICAL GxP RISK: Automated approval workflows in Veeva Vault for GxP-regulated content must maintain validated state and electronic signature compliance (21 CFR Part 11, EU Annex 11). Automated document approval without proper electronic signature cannot replace required human review for regulated documents. Automated changes to validated Vault configurations require change control.

auth.notes: Veeva Vault uses session ID authentication via username/password or OAuth 2.0 for enterprise SSO. Application role-based access control. Java SDK (Vault Java SDK) for server-side code. Vault Java SDK for Vault actions and triggers. No native webhooks — scheduled jobs for data extraction. Veeva Developer Portal for documentation. Vault Loader for bulk data migration.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual enterprise subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription; Vault application (Clinical, Quality, Regulatory, Safety) separately priced
pricing.notes: Pleasanton, California. Veeva Systems. NYSE: VEEV. Founded 2007. $2.7B+ annual revenue. Life sciences cloud market leader. 1,600+ life sciences customers. Vault content management platform and Vault CRM. Purpose-built for pharma, biotech, and medtech. 21 CFR Part 11 and EU Annex 11 compliance. Competes with OpenText and Documentum for regulated content management.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API session; bulk operations via Vault Loader; async operations for large datasets

known_agent_gotchas: CRITICAL GxP RISK: Automated document approval in regulated Vaults must comply with 21 CFR Part 11 — AI agent approvals require validated electronic signature workflows
Vault validation state — automation in validated Vaults requires change control; coordinate with QA for any API-driven workflow changes
Session-based auth — session IDs expire; implement re-authentication; prefer OAuth2 SSO for longer-lived automation sessions
Vault Java SDK — Veeva recommends Java SDK for server-side Vault code; Java-based Vault actions and triggers for complex automation
Cross-vault operations — Vault cross-linking allows data relationships across Vault applications; understand data model before querying
No native webhooks — implement scheduled jobs via Vault's built-in scheduling or external polling; event-driven integration via Vault integration framework
agent_readiness.security_notes: Life sciences content management. SOC2, ISO27001, GxP, 21-CFR-Part-11. Session/OAuth2. US/EU. Regulated document and clinical data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Life sciences GxP content. SOC2, ISO27001, GxP, 21-CFR-Part-11. Session/OAuth2. US/EU. Regulated document and clinical data.
